Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

The city library will close at 3 PM on Friday instead of the usual 8 PM because of scheduled electrical maintenance. Normal hours resume Saturday.
ground_truth: Key facts: library closes early at 3 PM on Friday; reason is electrical maintenance; normal hours resume Saturday.

tier0.answer_text: The city library will close early at 3 PM on Friday due to scheduled electrical maintenance, and it will return to its normal 8 PM closing time on Saturday.

tier3.answer_text: The city library will close early at 3 PM on Friday due to electrical maintenance, and normal hours will resume on Saturday.

tier1.answer_text: The city library will close at 3 PM on Friday instead of the usual 8 PM for scheduled electrical maintenance, resuming normal hours on Saturday.

tier2.answer_text: The city library will close early at 3 PM on Friday due to electrical maintenance, with normal hours resuming on Saturday.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

Registration for the spring marathon opens March 1st and costs $40. Participants who register before March 15th receive a free race shirt.
ground_truth: Key facts: registration opens March 1st; costs $40; registering before March 15th gets a free shirt.

tier0.answer_text: The spring marathon registration begins on March 1st with a $40 fee, and anyone who signs up by March 15th will receive a complimentary race shirt.

tier3.answer_text: Spring marathon registration opens March 1st for $40, with early registrants before March 15th receiving a free race shirt.

tier1.answer_text: Registration for the spring marathon opens March 1st and costs $40, with participants who register before March 15th receiving a free race shirt.

tier2.answer_text: Spring marathon registration opens on March 1st for $40, and those who sign up before March 15th will receive a free race shirt.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

Researchers at the institute tested three battery chemistries over 2,000 charge cycles. The lithium-iron-phosphate cells retained 91% capacity, outperforming the nickel-manganese-cobalt cells at 78% and the older lithium-cobalt-oxide design at 64%. The team attributes the gap mainly to cathode degradation at high temperatures.
ground_truth: Key facts: three battery chemistries tested over 2,000 cycles; LFP retained 91%, NMC 78%, LCO 64%; gap attributed to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

tier0.answer_text: The study found that after 2,000 charge cycles, lithium‑iron‑phosphate batteries kept 91% of their capacity, far surpassing nickel‑manganese‑cobalt (78%) and older lithium‑cobalt‑oxide cells (64%). The researchers say the performance gap is mainly due to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

tier3.answer_text: After 2,000 charge cycles, lithium-iron-phosphate batteries retained 91% capacity, significantly outperforming nickel-manganese-cobalt (78%) and lithium-cobalt-oxide (64%) cells, a difference the researchers primarily attribute to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

tier1.answer_text: Researchers found that lithium-iron-phosphate cells retained 91% capacity after 2,000 cycles, significantly outperforming nickel-manganese-cobalt (78%) and lithium-cobalt-oxide (64%) cells, with the performance gap attributed mainly to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

tier2.answer_text: Researchers tested three battery chemistries over 2,000 charge cycles, finding that lithium-iron-phosphate cells retained the most capacity (91%), outperforming nickel-manganese-cobalt and lithium-cobalt-oxide cells. The performance gap is attributed mainly to cathode degradation at high temperature

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

The quarterly report shows revenue grew 12% year over year to $4.8 million, driven mostly by the subscription segment, while hardware sales declined 5%. Operating costs rose 8%, leaving net margin roughly flat at 14%.
ground_truth: Key facts: revenue grew 12% YoY to $4.8M; subscriptions drove growth; hardware declined 5%; costs rose 8%; net margin flat at about 14%.

tier0.answer_text: The report shows revenue up 12% YoY to $4.8 million, mainly from subscriptions, while hardware sales fell 5%. Operating costs rose 8%, keeping the net margin roughly steady at 14%.

tier3.answer_text: Revenue rose 12% year over year to $4.8 million, driven by subscription growth despite a 5% decline in hardware sales, but an 8% increase in operating costs kept net margin flat at 14%.

tier1.answer_text: Revenue grew 12% year over year to $4.8 million, driven by subscription growth that offset a 5% hardware sales decline, but an 8% increase in operating costs left net margin flat at 14%.

tier2.answer_text: Revenue grew 12% year over year to $4.8 million, led by subscriptions but offset by a 5% decline in hardware sales, while an 8% rise in operating costs kept net margin roughly flat at 14%.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

The committee initially proposed relocating the bus depot to the north site, but after the flood-risk assessment came back unfavorable, it reversed course and recommended the east site, despite that option costing $2 million more and requiring an extra year of construction. The final vote was 6 to 3.
ground_truth: Key facts: committee reversed from north site to east site; reason was unfavorable flood-risk assessment; east site costs $2M more and takes one extra year; vote was 6 to 3.

tier0.answer_text: The committee first favored moving the bus depot to the north site, but a poor flood‑risk assessment led them to switch to the east site, even though it costs $2 million more and adds a year of construction; the final vote was 6‑3.

tier3.answer_text: The committee reversed its initial proposal for the north site after an unfavorable flood-risk assessment, instead recommending the east site despite its $2 million higher cost and extra year of construction, with a final vote of 6 to 3.

tier1.answer_text: After an unfavorable flood-risk assessment ruled out the north site, the committee voted 6 to 3 to recommend the east site for the bus depot, despite it costing $2 million more and requiring an additional year of construction.

tier2.answer_text: After an unfavorable flood-risk assessment for the north site, the committee voted 6–3 to recommend the east site for the bus depot, despite it costing $2 million more and requiring an extra year of construction.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

Although early trials suggested the drug reduced symptoms by 30%, the larger phase-3 study found only an 11% reduction, which fell short of statistical significance. The company will not seek approval and instead plans a reformulated version targeting a narrower patient group.
ground_truth: Key facts: early trials showed 30% reduction; phase-3 showed only 11%, not statistically significant; company will not seek approval; plans a reformulated version for a narrower group.

tier0.answer_text: The drug showed a modest 30% symptom reduction in early trials, but a larger phase‑3 study only achieved an 11% reduction that was not statistically significant. Consequently, the company will abandon approval efforts and develop a reformulated version for a more limited patient population.

tier3.answer_text: A drug that initially showed a 30% symptom reduction in early trials failed to achieve statistical significance in a larger phase-3 study with only an 11% reduction, prompting the company to forgo approval and instead develop a reformulated version for a narrower patient group.

tier1.answer_text: Although early trials showed a 30% symptom reduction, the phase-3 study found only an 11% reduction that was not statistically significant, so the company will not seek approval and instead plans to reformulate the drug for a narrower patient group.

tier2.answer_text: The drug failed to achieve statistically significant symptom reduction in its phase-3 trial, prompting the company to abandon its approval efforts. Instead, the company plans to develop a reformulated version targeting a narrower patient group.

ground_truth: Key facts: early trials showed 30% reduction; phase-3 showed only 11%, not statistically significant; company will not seek approval; plans a reformulated version for a narrower group.

tier0.answer_text: The initial trials showed a 30% symptom reduction, but a larger phase‑3 study only achieved an 11% reduction that wasn’t statistically significant. Consequently, the company will abandon approval efforts and develop a reformulated drug aimed at a more specific patient population.

prompt:
Summarize the following passage in 1-2 sentences:

Researchers at the institute tested three battery chemistries over 2,000 charge cycles. The lithium-iron-phosphate cells retained 91% capacity, outperforming the nickel-manganese-cobalt cells at 78% and the older lithium-cobalt-oxide design at 64%. The team attributes the gap mainly to cathode degradation at high temperatures.
ground_truth: Key facts: three battery chemistries tested over 2,000 cycles; LFP retained 91%, NMC 78%, LCO 64%; gap attributed to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

tier0.answer_text: The researchers found that after 2,000 charge cycles, lithium‑iron‑phosphate batteries kept 91% of their capacity, far surpassing nickel‑manganese‑cobalt cells at 78% and older lithium‑cobalt‑oxide cells at 64%, a difference they attribute mainly to cathode degradation at high temperatures.

prompt:

Write a Python function is_even(n) that returns True if the integer n is even. Return the complete function in a ```python code block. Do not include example usage.

tier0.answer_text:
```python
def is_even(n: int) -> bool:
    """
    Return True if the given integer n is even, otherwise False.

    Parameters
    ----------
    n : int
        The integer to check.

    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if n is even, False otherwise.
    """
    return n % 2 == 0
```
